summary.en: A result clause explains the outcome of the main clause. Build it with 'so that', or the bracketing structures 'so + adjective/adverb + that' and 'such + noun phrase + that'. Don't confuse 'so that' with the coordinating conjunction 'so'.
summary.zh: 结果状语从句解释主句动作产生的结果。可用 so that 引导，也可用 so…that… 和 such…that… 的“包夹”结构。注意区分 so that 与并列连词 so。

heading.zh: so that 引导结果从句

body.en: A result adverbial clause can be introduced by 'so that'. The clause it introduces explains the situation in the main clause — it is the outcome the main action produces. Tell it apart from the purpose clause: a result reading is the only one that makes sense when the outcome is not something anyone could intend.
body.zh: 结果状语从句可以由 so that 构成。它引导的从句用来解释说明主句的情况，是主句动作产生的结果。要把它和目的从句区分开：当那个结果根本不可能是有意为之时，就只能按结果来理解。

concepts.sourceRef: 结果状语从句也可以由so that构成

heading.zh: so that（从句） vs. so（并列）

body.en: The conjunction 'so' also means 'therefore / and the result is'. But 'so' joins two clauses of equal status into a compound sentence, while 'so that' introduces a subordinate adverbial clause that merely explains the main clause's result. Same logic as the 'because' vs. 'for' contrast.
body.zh: 连词 so 同样表示“因此、结果是”。但 so 连接两个地位同等的句子，构成并列复合句；而 so that 引导的是从属的副词从句，只起解释主句结果的作用。这跟 because 和 for 的区别是一个道理。

body.en: Two bracketing structures express result by degree. Use 'so' before an adjective or adverb: 'so many carrots that…'. Use 'such' before a noun phrase: 'such a tasty carrot that…'. The 'that' clause states the result. If you insist on 'so' before a noun, the adjective must come first and the article moves after it: 'so tasty a carrot that…' — grammatical but awkward.
body.zh: 还有两个“包夹”结构按程度表达结果。so 后接形容词或副词：so many carrots that…。such 后接名词（短语）：such a tasty carrot that…。that 从句给出结果。如果一定要在名词前用 so，必须把形容词提前、冠词后置：so tasty a carrot that…，虽合语法但很别扭。

concepts.sourceRef: such后要接名词而不是形容词

examples.text: I ate a lot of carrots for lunch so that I wasn't hungry at all in the afternoon.

annotations.quote: I ate a lot of carrots for lunch
I wasn't hungry at all in the afternoon

note.en: 'So that' introduces the result of eating a lot of carrots.
note.zh: so that 引出“吃了很多胡萝卜”的结果。

examples.text: The temperature was low so that the lake froze.

note.en: Only a result reading works: the cold temperature wasn't aiming to freeze the lake — it just did.
note.zh: 只能按结果理解：气温低不是为了让湖结冰，而是导致湖结冰。

examples.text: I like this video so I'll give it a thumbs up.

annotations.quote: I'll give it a thumbs up

note.en: Here 'so' joins two equal clauses into a compound sentence — not a subordinate result clause.
note.zh: 这里 so 把两个同等的句子连成并列复合句——不是从属的结果从句。

examples.text: I ate so many carrots for lunch that I felt sick afterwards.

note.en: 'So' modifies the quantity word 'many'; the 'that' clause gives the result.
note.zh: so 修饰数量词 many；that 从句给出结果。

examples.text: It was such a tasty carrot that I ate it all at once.

annotations.quote: I ate it all at once

note.en: 'Such' precedes the noun phrase 'a tasty carrot'; the result is eating it all at once.
note.zh: such 接名词短语 a tasty carrot；结果是一下子全吃光。

examples.text: It was so tasty a carrot that I ate it all at once.

annotations.quote: I ate it all at once

note.en: With 'so' before a noun, the adjective comes first and the article moves after it: 'so tasty a carrot'. Grammatical but awkward.
note.zh: so 用在名词前时，形容词提前、冠词后置：so tasty a carrot。合语法但别扭。

prompt.en: Which word follows 'such' in a result structure?
prompt.zh: 在结果结构中，such 后面接什么？

explain.en: 'Such' takes a noun phrase ('such a tasty carrot'); 'so' is the one that takes an adjective or adverb.
explain.zh: such 接名词短语（such a tasty carrot）；接形容词或副词的是 so。

prompt.en: Complete the result clause: 'I ate ____ many carrots that I felt sick afterwards.'
prompt.zh: 补全结果从句：'I ate ____ many carrots that I felt sick afterwards.'

explain.en: Before the quantity word 'many' you use 'so': 'so many carrots that…'.
explain.zh: 在数量词 many 前用 so：so many carrots that…。

prompt.en: Which sentence uses a subordinate result clause (not two equal clauses)?
prompt.zh: 哪一句用的是从属的结果从句（而非两个同等的句子）？

text.en: I like this video so I'll give it a thumbs up.
text.zh: I like this video so I'll give it a thumbs up.

text.en: The temperature was low so that the lake froze.
text.zh: The temperature was low so that the lake froze.

text.en: I was tired so I went to bed.
text.zh: I was tired so I went to bed.

explain.en: 'So that' introduces a subordinate result clause; plain 'so' (a, c) just joins two equal clauses into a compound sentence.
explain.zh: so that 引导从属结果从句；单独的 so（a、c）只是把两个同等句子连成并列句。

quizzes.sentence: It was so tasty carrot that I ate it all at once.
quizzes.fix: It was such a tasty carrot that I ate it all at once.

explain.en: Before the noun phrase 'a tasty carrot' use 'such'. (To keep 'so' you would have to reorder it to 'so tasty a carrot'.)
explain.zh: 名词短语 a tasty carrot 前要用 such。（若坚持用 so，必须改写成 so tasty a carrot。）

title.en: A carrot lunch and its results
title.zh: 一顿胡萝卜午餐的结果

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: I ate a lot of carrots for lunch so that I wasn't hungry at all in the afternoon.

annotations.quote: I wasn't hungry at all in the afternoon

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: Really? I ate so many carrots that I felt sick afterwards.

note.zh: so + many … that — 按程度表结果。

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: It was such a tasty carrot that I ate it all at once.

annotations.quote: I ate it all at once

note.en: 'Such + noun phrase + that' — result by degree.
note.zh: such + 名词短语 + that — 按程度表结果。

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: I like your carrots so I'll give you a thumbs up.

note.en: Plain 'so' just joins two equal clauses — not a result clause.
note.zh: 单独的 so 只连接两个同等句子——不是结果从句。
